message.content: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities to crash applications or systems, leading to Endpoint Denial of Service (DoS). This can deny availability to users and potentially cause a persistent DoS condition if the system automatically restarts and is re-exploited. Exploitation can involve known or zero-day vulnerabilities, affecting dependent applications and systems, and may result in data destruction, firmware corruption, or service stoppage. Such attacks can severely impact the availability of critical information and services. For instance, Industroyer uses a custom DoS tool targeting Siemens SIPROTEC devices via hardcoded IP addresses. Mitigation strategies include regular patching and updates, implementing robust intrusion detection systems, and employing network segmentation to limit the spread of attacks.
